notes: Generated directly by the parent Codex image generation path from the existing Shen Weisang costume board, global style, material palette, Canyang palette, and border snowline material references.
Rejected earlier variants that displayed too much old-empire badge language on the shoulder, pouch, or material strip.
Final board preserves the user-corrected rule: Shen Weisang is a human remnant under Suming rule, so the half relay token is hidden and no old-empire insignia is worn openly.

Final board preserves the user-specified gold-dark pattern behavior: dormant dark-gold cracks and an explicit activated state group with warm gold and gold-red internal glow in eye, shoulder, seam, and charred-hem detail panels.
First generated attempt was rejected and archived because Yan Nanzhi read too masculine and included a large background emblem-like diagram.
Later face revisions were archived after the user requested stronger real-person recognizability and a return toward the first-version v04 face identity.
The v005 prior final was archived after the user identified the face as still reading closer to guazi/melon-seed than true oval.

Current final uses the first-version v04 face sheet as the strongest identity reference while correcting the face into a true classical oval: softly rounded forehead, balanced midface width, fuller living cheeks, gradual cheek-to-jaw curve, short rounded non-pointed chin, heavier guarded eyelids, realistic skin texture, and stronger identity.
Final board preserves existing costume, prop, palette, and hidden-symbol logic without redesigning the locked Zhaoming emblem.

Generated directly by the parent Codex image generation path using existing visible project character boards, Shen Zhaomian child-relic board, global style, Canyang palette, and Qingming institutional palette as references.
Final board preserves three states: village herb child, post-fire missing-child anchor, and Qingming Court child-white identifier.

Post-fire state is dirtier than ideal for some shots; downstream prompts should keep distress localized and preserve complete child garment structure.
